content.full_text:
California
Fair POlitical
Practices Cornmission
May 7, 1990
David L. zaltsman
Deputy county counse]
Napa county
1195 Third street, Room 301
Napa, CA 94559-300L
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-293
Dear Mr. zaltsman:
This is in response to your letter requesting advice on
behalf of Local Agency Formation Conmission chainoan Thomas Jordan
regarding his responsibilities under the conflict-of-j-nterest
pr6visiois of the Political Reform Act (the nasl'r1.1/
OUESTTON
May commissioner Jordan participate in Local Agency Formation
Comrnission decisions concerning the incorporation of the American
canyon region of Napa county if a land owner j-n the reqion has
been a source of income or promised income to the commissioner's
ernp loyer?
CONCLUSION
Absent facts indicati,ng a foreseeable material- financial-
impact on the commissioner's employer as a result of the decision,
commissioner Jordan may participate in the decision to incorporate
American Canyon.
FACTS
The Napa Loca1 Agency Formation conmission (hereafter
'tI"AFco") is currently considering a petition for the reorganj-za-
tion of the American canyon region of Napa county. The petition
requests LAFco approval for the incorporation of American canyon.
If LAFCo approves the petition, the petition will be subnitted to
L/ covernment code Sections 8100o-9Lo15. ALI statutory refer-
ences are to the Governnent Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Com-
mission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. Al-l- references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the Cal-ifornia Code of Regulations.
428 J street, suite 8oo . P.o. Box 8oz rD sacramento cA 958o4.o8o7 . (916)322.5660


File No. A-90-293
Page 2
the Napa County Board of Supervisors for their consideration and
ultimately the public of Napa county to be voted on in an elec-
tion.
The unincorporated area of Napa County is subject to a growth
control initiative, 
Measure A, which severely restricts the number
of residential buiLding pernits that nay be issued in any given
year. If Amerj,can Canyon is incorporated, it will no longer be
subject to the restrictions on residential building perrnits.
Anerican canyon contains 80 parcels and 6 percent of the total
population of the county.
Commissioner Jordan i.s the chairman of the Napa county LAFco.
Cornmissioner Jordan is a professional real estate appraiser
ernployed by a firrn that does work in the American canyon area.
currently, the firn has contracted to do appraisals for an
individual- who owns property in the American canyon region of the
county. The firm will be compensated on a time and expenses basis
which is not contingent on the incorporation of American Canyon.
Cornmissioner Jordan is compensated as an employee of the firn
only in relation to the work he actually performs. Commissioner
Jordan is not invol-ved in the appraisal of the property in ques-
tion.
ANALYSI S
Sectj.on 87100 prohibits any public official 
from makingr,
participating in naking, or otherwise using his or her official
position to influence a lrovernmental decision in lrhich the of-
ficial 
has a financial interest. 
A I'public official" 
is defined
in Section 82048 and Regulation 18700 (copy enclosed) as every
natural person who is a member, officer, enployee, or consultant
of a state or local government agency. Thj.s definition includes
menbers of the Napa County LAFCO.
Section 87103 specifies that a public official 
has a
financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decision will have a material financial effect,
distinguishable from the effect on the public AeneraLly, on the
officiaL or a member of his or her i'nrnediate fanily or on:
(c) Any source of income, other than gifts and
other than loans by a conmercial lending institu-
tion in the regmlar course of busj-ness on terns
available to the public without regard to official
status, aggregating two hundred fifty dollars
($250) or more j.n value provided to, received by or
promised to the pubJ.ic official 
within 12 months
prior to the tirne when the decision is rnade.


File No. A-90-293
Page 3
(d) Any busj,ness entity in which the public
official 
is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of manageEent.
section 871o3 (c) and (d) .
You stated that Commissioner Jordan is an enployee of a real,
estate appraisal f ino that does business in the American canyon
region of Napa county. Ilolrever, you also stated that the commis-
sioner is only compensated as an ernployee of the firm in relation
to the work he actually performs, and that the commissj.oner is not
involved in the appraisal of the property in question. Thus, it
appears unlikety that there will be any financial effect resulting
fion the A.nerican canyon decj.sion on the conmissioner personally.
However, the analysis with respect to conflicts of interest
does not end with effects on the conmissioner Personalfy.
Commissioner Jordan's employer is also an economic j.nterest of the
coumissioner, because of the empLoyer-enployee relationship
(Section 87103 (d) ) and because the firn has been a source of
income to the conmissioner of $250 or more in the last 12 months.
(section 87103(c).) Thus, Conmissioner Jordan nay not participate
in the American canyon incorporation decision if it is foreseeable
that the decision will have a material financial effect on his
enployer.
whether the financial consequences of a decj-sion are reason-
ably foreseeable at the tiue a governmental decision is made
depends on the facts of each particular case. An effect is
considered reasonably foreseeable if there is a substantial
Iikelihood that it will occur. certai.nty is not required.
Holrever, if an effect is only a mere possibility, 
it is not
reasonably foreseeable. (In re Thorner (1975) 1 fPPc ops. 198,
copy enclosed. )
In addition, the foreseeable effect on a source of income
must also be naterial to require disqualification. 
The Cornmission
has adopted differing guidelines to deternine whether an effect is
material, depending on the specific circumstances of each decj.-
sion. For example, r.rhere a source of incone is directly before
the official's 
lgency,2/ as an applicant or the subject of the
2/ 
A source of income is directly before LAFco when the source
initiates the proceeding by filing an application, c1aim, appeal-,
or similar request, or is a naned party in, or the subject of, the
proceeding. A person or business entity is the subject of a
proceeding if a decision j.nvolves the issuance, renei{al., approval,
denial or revocation of any license, pernit, or other entitlement
to, or contract with, the subject person or business entity.
(Regulation La702.1 (b) . )


File No. A-90-293
iPaqe 4
decision, Regulation 18702.1(a) (copy enclosed) provides that the
effect of the decision on a source of income is deemed material
and disqual ification is required. 
(combs Advice Letter, No.
A-a9-L77 , copy enclosed. )
Where the source of incone is not directly before the
official's 
agency, as in your facts, Regulations 18702.2 and
1,8'102.6 (copies enclosed) apply. whether the indirect effect on a
business entity is material depends on the financial size of the
business entity. 
Regrulation 7a7o2.2 provi.des that for a
relatively snall business entity, the indirect effect of a deci-
sion is Daterial where:
(1) The decision will result in an increase
or decrease in the gross revenues for a fiscal year
of $10,000 or more; or
(2) The decision will result in the business
entity incurring or avoiding additional expenses or
reducing or eliminating existing expenses for a
fiscal year in the auount of $2,500 or Eore; or
(3) The decision wj-II result in the i.ncrease
or decrease in the value of assets or liabilities
of $10,0o0 or nore.
You stated that the firn's fee from the property owner lras
not contingent on the incorporation of American canyon.
Consequently, while the decision may prove beneficial to the
property owner, it hri1l not financially affect the commissioner's
employer. Thus, absent facts indicating a foreseeable financial
inpact on the comui.ssioner's employer as a resu.Lt of the decision,
we lrouId conclude that Com,nissioner Jordan may participate in the
decision to incorporate Auerican canyon.
If you have any further questions regarding this uratter,
ptease feel free to contact ne at (916) 322-59oL.
Sincerely,
Kathryn E. Donovan
l counsel
,}ax!
John w. wallace
counsel, Legal Divis j,on
KED: JWW: plh
Enclosures

American Canyon.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May commissioner Jordan participate in Local Agency Formation
Comrnission decisions concerning the incorporation of the American
canyon region of Napa county if a land owner j-n the reqion has
been a source of income or promised income to the commissioner's
ernp loyer?

CONCLUSION: Absent facts indicati,ng a foreseeable material- financial-
impact on the commissioner's employer as a result of the decision,
commissioner Jordan may participate in the decision to incorporate
American Canyon.
